title: Add org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.podman extension for Flatpak
body:
### Feature request description

This will allow PhpStorm and other IDEs to use podman as an extension.

I'm required by Flathub to contact you, as I did submit a SDK myself.

### Suggest potential solution

Create `org.freedesktop.Sdk.Extension.podman`.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

It is possible to call host commands, but this is discourage, as you escape the sandbox.

### Additional context

I use podman for devcontainer on PhpStorm and VSCode.

comments.body:
This sounds like a packaging thing that everybody could do so I doubt this is something we upstream maintainers would be interest to pick up as extra work.

It also unclear what behavior a user would want, generally you need to run podman on the host for proper functioning, i.e. user namespaces, it needs access to its storage location, config files and so on.
If you want to run the full podman within the flatpak sandbox then I am not sure how this would work as flatpak only has one uid mapped into is userns.

My comment was meant for the main `podman` binary, `podman-remote` is just an http client for the service so sure if you have a service running on the host or elsewhere and you just need to point podman-remote at it then it should work fine from within the sandbox.

As far maintaining an flatpak extension I have no experience and no interested in doing so, I cannot speak for other maintainers of course but I do not think there would be anyone else here that likes to do that additional work.

@Luap99 It's indeed used with the already locally provided podman host. Not tested myself, but I think even remote podman hosts can work with `podman-remote`?

I fully understand. In most cases it shouldn't be needed to do any extra work for Flatpaks as long as it can communicate correctly I think.
I'm pretty sure myself that the podman binary won't work properly inside of the flatpak sandbox, if thats what is being requested here. User namespaces are _the_ basis for this

@tulilirockz The benefit would be to have a podman binary for basic tasks (maybe?).

What about podman-compose? The main benefit would be to at least parse compose files.

> I'm pretty sure myself that the podman binary won't work properly inside of the flatpak sandbox, if thats what is being requested here. User namespaces are _the_ basis for this

Well that is what I said in my comments and the ask is for podman-remote not podman.

> What about podman-compose? The main benefit would be to at least parse compose files.

podman-compose just shells out to `podman` AFAIK, it is a separated project not maintained by us. You should be able to use `docker-compose` again the remote socket just like podman-remote though.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
